name: 徐芳達
current_positions: Deputy Chairman of MAS Board of Directors
Member of Parliament (Toa Payoh West-Thomson, Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC)

Business Administration (MBA) - Newmont Australia Prize for Most Outstanding MBA Graduate

Principal Private Secretary to Minister Mentor Lee Kuan Yew

Ministry of Communications and Information / Ministry of Health

Ministry of Transport / Ministry of Finance

person_relationships.description: Served as Principal Private Secretary to Lee Kuan Yew (2008-2011); was one of eight pallbearers at Lee's state funeral in 2015
Jeffrey Siow succeeded Chee as Acting Minister for Transport when Chee moved to National Development
Succeeded Desmond Lee as Minister for National Development in May 2025
Succeeded S. Iswaran as Acting Minister for Transport after Iswaran was investigated by CPIB for corruption
Along with Ong Ye Kung, was seen dining with convicted money launderer Su Haijin in May 2025 controversy

political_stances.stance_content: Wrote letter to Straits Times arguing it would be 'stupid for any Singapore agency or NTU to advocate the learning of dialects, which must be at the expense of English and Mandarin'; supported Speak Mandarin Campaign
Defended government against Workers' Party's GST allegations, saying it was an attack on PAP government's integrity; WP MP Sylvia Lim later acknowledged she 'may have been wrong'
Alleged WP NCMP Leon Perera made false accusations about Mediacorp partisan edits; Perera later apologised in Parliament

major_achievements.achievement: Awarded Newmont Australia Prize for Most Outstanding MBA Graduate
One of eight pallbearers at Lee Kuan Yew's state funeral
Won Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC with 73.59% of vote in political debut
Re-elected in Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC with 67.26% of vote
Took over as Acting Minister for Transport during S. Iswaran corruption crisis, stabilising the ministry
Promoted to full Minister for Transport and Second Minister for Finance
Led PAP team in Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC as anchor minister to victory with 75% of vote against SPP in GE2025
Appointed Minister for National Development in Second Lawrence Wong Cabinet

biography_summary: Chee Hong Tat (born 4 November 1973) is a Singaporean politician and former civil servant who has served as Minister for National Development since May 2025. Educated at The Chinese High School and Raffles JC, he graduated from UC Berkeley with dual highest honours degrees (Electrical Engineering/CS and Economics) on an Overseas Merit Scholarship, and an MBA from University of Adelaide where he was Most Outstanding Graduate. A career civil servant, he served as Principal Private Secretary to Lee Kuan Yew (2008-2011) and CEO of the Energy Market Authority (2011-2014). He entered politics in 2015 and has held portfolios across Health, Communications, Trade, Education, Transport, and Finance. He took over as Transport Minister during the S. Iswaran corruption crisis (2023-2025), then moved to National Development in 2025. He is Deputy Chairman of MAS Board and Assistant Treasurer of PAP. Married with four children.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with detailed civil service career (MHA, MOF, MOT, MOE), PPS to Lee Kuan Yew role, EMA CEO tenure, full ministerial progression, dialect controversy stance, GST integrity defense, MAS Board role, GIC ISC membership, Iswaran succession context, GE2025 results (75% as anchor minister), and Su Haijin dining controversy.
